items.q: According to the inverted-U hypothesis, when is performance best?
items.correct: At an optimal moderate level of arousal.
items.why: Students assume 'more psyched up = better'. The inverted-U shows performance rises with arousal up to an optimum, then DECLINES with excess arousal (the athlete chokes). The optimum varies by skill.

items.correct: Information the performer senses from their own body during/after movement.
items.why: Students reverse the meaning. Intrinsic = from WITHIN (kinesthetic awareness, feel); extrinsic = from OUTSIDE (coach feedback, video, statistics). Experienced performers rely more on intrinsic feedback.

items.q: Is a tennis serve a closed or open skill?
items.correct: Closed - performed in a predictable, self-paced environment.
items.misconception: Open - because tennis is a fast-paced sport.
items.why: Students judge by the sport, not the moment. The serve happens at the server's pace with a stationary ball - closed. The return of serve is open. Skill classification depends on the SPECIFIC action's environment.
Correct answer: 1896. “1776” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Year of the first modern Olympic Games?
items.why: The real figure is 1896. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
